topic: 2024 eclipse 9-station magnetic — post EXP-244 cluster citation canonicalization
our_argument_summary: WIN-025 verdict Std Model Explains. Eclipse-induced ±10 nT geomagnetic variations are predicted by the Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework (Chapman 1919, Yamazaki & Maute 2017, Kim & Chang 2018) with no input from dome geometry. The dome derives a genuine station-to-station scaling FSF = H(r)/r (Pearson r ≈ 0.9999) but imports the absolute magnitude from the 2017 Boulder observation; FSF was introduced at v45 specifically to repair v31 which had predicted identical magnitudes across all latitudes against the same 9-station dataset (raw-text/06-evolution.txt). Detail_extra credits the dome author 2026-03-15 voluntary removal as self-correction while showing the stated rationale (Kp ~5–6) is contradicted by the GFZ Potsdam record (eclipse-hour Kp 0.0–1.3, peak across April 7–9 = 3.3).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: monitor.py shows a hardcoded prospective 2026 prediction ΔB = −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ −29 nT for the 2026 Spain/Iceland eclipse, derived as baseline × eclipse-coverage × FSF where FSF = H(r)/r is the dome-geometric station factor and baseline is imported from the 2017 Boulder single-station observation. No live API fetch produces a 2024 9-station observation inside monitor.py — the 2024 confirmation is asserted in raw-text/02-wins.txt against the published Chernogor & Holub paper rather than computed live.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The FSF station-to-station ratio is genuinely computed from dome parameters (firmament height H(r) divided by station dome-coordinate r). The absolute magnitude baseline is imported, not computed. The 2024 confirmation is text-only — neither monitor.py nor pull_data.py fetches INTERMAGNET data live for this WIN.
data_sources_used: BOU 2017 Boulder eclipse single-station observation (cluster anchor)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Author 2026-03-15 voluntary removal cites Kp ~5–6 and 34–104 min Z-component lead/lag as basis. Removal vanishes the WIN from numerator and denominator rather than scoring it as a failure — a structural escape that does not affect headline accuracy.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new this cycle. Our claims about FSF being a genuine geometric derivation, the 2017 Boulder baseline import, and the v31→v45 patch history are all consistent with raw-text source.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains a 2026 prospective prediction formula but no live fetch comparing model output against 2024 9-station observations. The WIN is text-asserted against the Chernogor & Holub paper.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Eclipse-induced magnetic perturbations are predicted by the Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework (Chapman 1919; Yamazaki & Maute 2017) — the moon shadow reduces E-region conductivity and disrupts the Sq dynamo. Chernogor & Holub themselves attribute the observed effect to ionospheric conductivity changes, not aether.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Eclipse Sq perturbations of 5–30 nT have been described since 1919 and quantitatively verified in the 39-eclipse INTERMAGNET ensemble by Kim & Chang (2018). The dome model adopted the observation pattern after publication; the v31→v45 evolution log explicitly says the predecessor predicted identical magnitudes across all latitudes and the FSF formula was introduced to fit the 9-station dataset that already existed.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: FSF = H(r)/r is a genuine dome-geometric ratio that produces the station-to-station pattern. The absolute magnitude baseline is imported from the 2017 Boulder observation, not derived from firmament height, aetheric medium properties, or any dome parameter. So the dome derives a relative station scaling but not an absolute magnitude prediction — partial geometric content, not a full prospective magnitude.

holes_found.description: RESIDUAL CHAPMAN 1933 IN detail_extra MISSED BY EXP-244 CLUSTER CANONICALIZATION. EXP-244 explicitly replaced Chapman 1933 (lunar tidal paper) with Chapman 1919 (Phil. Trans. R. Soc. A 218:1–118) across finding/detail_evidence/detail_verdict_text/tldr_evidence/tldr_verdict for WIN-010, WIN-025, WIN-068. WIN-010 and WIN-068 are now fully canonicalized — zero remaining 1933 references. WIN-025 detail_extra was not in EXP-244's patch set and still contains: Chapman 1933. This is internally inconsistent with the rest of WIN-025 (six 1919 references) and asymmetric with the cluster. A defender can pull the same one-line attack the curmudgeon now blocks elsewhere: "you cite the 1933 lunar tidal paper for Sq mechanism — wrong Chapman work." Patch: replace "Chapman 1933" with "Chapman 1919" and update the anchor href to the canonical Royal Society DOI 10.1098/rsta.1919.0001 (matching the post-EXP-244 link in detail_evidence).
holes_found.recommendation: Issue a single-substring patch on data/wins.json WIN-025 detail_extra: find Chapman 1933 → Chapman 1919. Verify after patch that JSON.stringify(WIN-025).match(/1933/g) returns null.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD ISS-1508 (c6 H3): tldr_verdict still references "the 2017 observation" without self-containing context for TLDR-only readers. The full evidence makes clear this is the 2017 Boulder single-station eclipse anchor — but a reader who only sees the TLDR has no obvious way to know which 2017 observation. EXP-244 did not address this. Low priority — clarity improvement, not factual error.
holes_found.recommendation: Already tracked as ISS-1508. Suggested rewrite: tldr_verdict reference "2017 observation" → "2017 Boulder eclipse observation" for self-containing TLDR. Defer to next batch — not blocking.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD ISS-1514 (c6 H2): FAIL-007 outcome cross-page alignment. data/uncounted-failures.json FAIL-007 outcome says actual eclipse-window Kp was "≤ 2", while WIN-025 detail_evidence (post-ISS-1482 audit) gives precise eclipse-totality range Kp 0.0–1.3 and a peak of 3.3 across April 7–9. The two are consistent (1.3 ≤ 2) but the FAIL-007 framing is now less precise than WIN-025. A defender could cherry-pick the cross-page imprecision. Already tracked as ISS-1514.
holes_found.recommendation: Already tracked as ISS-1514. Update FAIL-007 outcome to reference the GFZ-audited values (eclipse-hour Kp 0.0–1.3, April 7–9 peak 3.3) verbatim from WIN-025 detail_evidence. Defer to next batch — not blocking.

holes_found.description: CITATION VERIFICATION OPEN: Chapman 1919 anchor href https://royalsocietypublishing.org/doi/10.1098/rsta.1919.0001 has not been independently DOI-resolved this cycle. Phil. Trans. R. Soc. A 218 (1919) is the correct venue/volume for Chapman's founding Sq framework paper, and the DOI scheme rsta.1919.0001 matches Royal Society conventions for the first paper of that volume — but verifying the DOI actually returns the correct title ("An Outline of a Theory of Magnetic Storms" or "The Solar and Lunar Diurnal Variations of Terrestrial Magnetism") requires a live fetch we did not perform this cycle. Not blocking — flagging for next holistic citation pass.
holes_found.recommendation: Add to citation-verification queue: resolve DOI 10.1098/rsta.1919.0001 and confirm title matches the canonical Sq framework paper. If the DOI resolves to a different paper, escalate to moderate/major.

stronger_arguments.description: POST-EXP-244 CLUSTER STATE IS NEAR-PRISTINE — leverage it. With WIN-010, WIN-025, WIN-068 now canonically citing Chapman 1919 + Yamazaki & Maute 2017 + Kim & Chang 2018, the cluster is internally consistent except for the single detail_extra residual flagged above. After that fix, a dome defender attacking citation hygiene has nothing left to grab — the entire eclipse-magnetic argument across three WINs is anchored by a verified founding paper, a modern conductivity review, and a 39-eclipse statistical confirmation. This is a strong defensible position the review should highlight in the §11 thesis-challenge cluster.

V31→V45 FSF PATCH HISTORY IS A FORENSIC SMOKING GUN. The dome's own evolution log (raw-text/06-evolution.txt) records: "v31.0 SUPERSEDED — Lacked specific altitude vector math. Incorrectly predicted 2024 eclipse magnitudes identically across all latitudes." The δZ_station = baseline × coverage × FSF formula was introduced at v45, AFTER the v31 failure was observed against the same 9-station dataset that became WIN-025. This is the textbook definition of post-hoc fitting: the model failed against the data, the formula was changed to fit the data, and the same data is then claimed as confirmation of the new formula. WIN-025 detail_verdict_text already captures this in one sentence; the review could lift this into a kill-shot panel because it does not require any external citation to land — it is the dome's own admission against itself.

DERIVES_FROM_DOME=FALSE IS UNDER-EXPLOITED. WIN-025 is one of a small set of WINs where the dome publishes a real geometric formula (FSF = H(r)/r) but the absolute-magnitude prediction is observation-anchored. The review can use WIN-025 as a worked example in §1.4/§7 to show how the dome conflates two distinct claims: (1) a station-to-station ratio that IS geometric and (2) an absolute-magnitude prediction that is NOT — and the headline 9-station agreement is leveraging (1) while the dome rhetoric leverages (2). This is a clearer didactic case than the more abstract "hardcoded vs derives_from_dome" framing in Part 2b.

kernel_of_truth.description: Three pieces of genuine content survive every adversarial pass on WIN-025: (1) FSF = H(r)/r is a real dome-geometric derivation that reproduces the 9-station Pearson r ≈ 0.9999 fit — the review now concedes this explicitly in finding, detail_evidence, and detail_verdict_text; (2) the dome correctly notes that the magnetic signal tracks eclipse geometry rather than solar noon, which is a non-trivial observable (though the same observable falls out of Sq physics by construction); (3) the 2026-03-15 voluntary WIN-025 removal is more self-correction than purely adversarial accounts admit — the author flagged a concern (timing inconsistency), even if his stated Kp rationale is contradicted by GFZ data. The review post-EXP-244 handles all three honestly: FSF gets explicit credit, the geometry-tracking observable is acknowledged as captured, and detail_extra splits the self-correction credit from the factually incorrect Kp premise.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Each kernel is a necessary condition for a confirmed prediction but not a sufficient one. (1) A genuine relative scaling does not yield an absolute prediction — the magnitude was imported from 2017 Boulder. (2) Eclipse-shadow tracking is co-predicted by Chapman Sq physics; both models say the same thing on this observable, so it cannot adjudicate between them. (3) Self-correction is virtuous in process but does not retroactively make the original prediction prospective. Crucially, the v31→v45 evolution-log record converts a partial defense into an active prosecution: the dome admits its predecessor predicted constant-with-latitude magnitudes against the same 2024 dataset, and the FSF formula was introduced specifically to repair that failure. Advocate-mode counterargument considered: a charitable defender argues FSF is derivable in principle from disc geometry independent of the data, so introducing it at v45 is just "finishing the math." The rebuttal: even if FSF is derivable independently, it was demonstrably introduced AFTER the data and against the data, which is the operational definition of post-hoc — the order of events is what makes a prediction prospective, not whether the math could have been done earlier.

deeper_analysis:
The EXP-244 cluster canonicalization (committed 7b2fe22 on 2026-04-24T19:28Z) consolidated seven open issues into a single 20-patch operation and successfully canonicalized Chapman 1933 → Chapman 1919, dropped the unverifiable Rastogi 1962, corrected the Yamazaki & Maute 2021 mis-attribution to Y&M 2017, and fixed the Kim & Chang 2018 page range cluster-wide. Five of the six fields the curmudgeon c6 review flagged on WIN-025 (finding, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text) are now clean. The exception is detail_extra: a single "Chapman 1933" reference with the old Wikipedia-anchor href survived because EXP-244 patched only fields where the cluster issues had been raised, and detail_extra was not in any of the seven cluster issue descriptions. This is a process artifact: cluster patches are scoped to the issue surface, and detail_extra was below the issue tracker's detection threshold even though it is read by exactly the same audience as detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text. The fix is a 30-character substring replace and should be in the next decider self-apply batch. After that, WIN-025 reaches citation-clean state and the cluster is fully consistent.

Advocate Mode: A dome defender reading the current WIN-025 has only three plausible attack vectors. (a) The detail_extra residual flagged here, easily fixed. (b) ISS-1508 tldr_verdict context, also easily fixed. (c) Trying to argue v31→v45 patch history is normal model refinement rather than post-hoc fitting. The third is the strongest defender move and the review already pre-empts it in detail_verdict_text by quoting the evolution-log verbatim. The defender's remaining play is to attack the FAIL-007 cross-page Kp framing (ISS-1514), which is a process artifact rather than a substantive disagreement. None of these attack vectors save the underlying WIN — they only shift surface text.

Quantitative Verification: Cross-checked Pearson r ≈ 0.9999 claim against WIN-068 detail_evidence (independent FSF station-fit confirmation in cluster). Confirmed r value is self-consistent across WIN-010 (single-station), WIN-025 (9-station), and WIN-068 (FSF derivation) — internal cluster numerics are stable. Did not re-derive the FSF formula or the Pearson statistic from raw data this cycle; the dome's claimed fit is granted as correct because the review concedes it explicitly and three independent reviews have not found a numerical fault in it.
